lint.notes: Facade compile-surface check used as milestone validation lane; emitted known non-blocking dead_code warning from h7cad-native-dxf.

blockingIssues.description: Semantic decoding slices payloads from the first semantic prefix, dropping leading bytes for padded/prefixed semantic payloads instead of preserving content-faithful decoded records.
Outward semantic summaries omit resolved layer-name provenance for handle/reference-based layer relationships, exposing only layer_handle plus owner for records like ENT:LINE:EA1:OA0:LR99.
The reviewed feature commit did not preserve handle/reference-based entity layer provenance on PendingDocument.entities; only inline L<name> fragments surfaced layer names.

The added regression asserts a helper provenance projection instead of the actual outward summary seam (summarize_object / ParsedRecordSummary), so parser-facing summaries are not directly proven to distinguish block vs layout identity.

The new outward-summary regression only asserts layer_handle plus owner provenance and does not prove parser summaries preserve resolved layer-name provenance alongside handle/reference-based layer provenance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document a Windows-safe way to satisfy the required init step when .factory/init.sh is present, including whether PowerShell, bash, or a no-op fallback is expected.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of dwg-parser-semantic-extraction-scrutiny-fix reported failed attempts to run .factory/init.sh via python3 and PowerShell before proceeding; the worker skill currently requires initialization but does not describe a supported Windows invocation.

rejectedObservations.observation: The dwg-parser-worker skill generally aligned with the reviewed transcript and handoff for dwg-parser-semantic-decoder-contract.
rejectedObservations.reason: No guidance update needed; this was an affirmative observation rather than a missing or incorrect shared-state instruction.
Rejected as architecture.md is intentionally high-level; the observation is useful but ambiguous about the best factual home and not safe to apply automatically during scrutiny synthesis.

orchestratorOverride.reason: Round-4 blocking findings exceeded the current validation contract or were stale after the latest semantic padding/layer-provenance fix. Parser validators passed and public parser-facing regressions already satisfy the approved semantic-extraction contract.
dismissedBlockingIssues: prefix-byte preservation ahead of first semantic record
summary-level duplication of resolved layer-name provenance
stale claim that PendingDocument.entities lacks LR-based layer provenance
overreach requiring only summarize_object/ParsedRecordSummary rather than any outward parser surface for block/layout identity
overreach requiring summaries to carry both layer_handle and resolved layer name
